SUMMARY.—In this paper we show the recognition of cultivated corn plants from the grasses or weeds surrounding the images. Convolutional neural networks have given the highest accuracies in object detection, but errors are produced when the corresponding features of the objects in different classes are similar. This usually occurs when the features of the objects in the image are too small to be clearly distinguished, causing the incorrect classification or localization of images. To solve this problem, we used the concept of “within the image” for the classification of images. The network is trained with a square image of the image itself, but in the test the square is extended to include the boundary of the immediate surroundings of the image. This is shown to be effective in the recognition of grass and grass-like weeds in corn fields. The error was reduced by nearly one-half by the use of this concept, and the accuracy was higher after a single training than after 50. The advantages of using the technique of “within the image” will probably be found especially useful in agriculture where the parts of various plants, such as the leaves, may look alike if the surrounding context is not taken into account.

Liquid state  Overhauser Effect  Dynamic Nuclear Polarization (ODNP) has
experienced a recent resurgence of interest. In particular, a  new  manifestation
of the ODNP measurement  measures the translational mobility of   water  within
5-10 \AA\  of an ESR-active spin  probe (i.e. the local translational diffusivity
D_{local} near an electron spin resonance active molecule). Such spin  probes,
typically stable nitroxide radicals,  have been attached to the  surface or
interior of macromolecules, including proteins, polymers, and  membrane
vesicles.  Despite the unique specificity of  this measurement, it requires only
a standard X-band (~10 GHz)  continuous wave (cw)  electron spin resonance  (ESR)
spectrometer, coupled with a standard nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR)
spectrometer. Here, we present a set of developments and corrections that  allow
us to improve  the  accuracy  of  quantitative  ODNP and apply it to samples more
than two orders of magnitude lower than were previously  feasible.

Referring image segmentation aims to segment the objects referred by a
natural language expression. 

Previous methods usually focus on designing an
implicit and recurrent feature interaction mechanism to fuse the
visual-linguistic features to directly generate the final segmentation mask
without explicitly modeling the localization information of the referent
instances. To tackle these problems, we view this task from another perspective
by decoupling it into a "Locate-Then-Segment" (LTS) scheme. Given a language
expression, people generally first perform attention to the corresponding
target image regions, then generate a fine segmentation mask about the object
based on its context. 

The LTS first extracts and fuses both visual and textual
features to get a cross-modal representation, then applies a cross-model
interaction on the visual-textual features to locate the referred object with
position prior, and finally generates the segmentation result with a
light-weight segmentation network. 

Our LTS is simple but surprisingly
effective. On three popular benchmark datasets, the LTS outperforms all the
previous state-of-the-art methods by a large margin (e.g., +3.2% on RefCOCO+
and +3.4% on RefCOCOg). 

In addition, our model is more interpretable with
explicitly locating the object, which is also proved by visualization
experiments. We believe this framework is promising to serve as a strong
baseline for referring image segmentation.

We study the impact of electrode band structure on transport through
single-molecule junctions by measuring the conductance of pyridine-based
molecules using Ag and Au electrodes. Our experiments are carried out using the
scanning tunneling microscope based break-junction technique and are Supported
by density functional theory based calculations. We find from both Experiments
and calculations that the coupling of the dominant transport orbital to the
metal is stronger for Au-based junctions when compared with ag-based junctions.
We attribute this difference to relativistic effects, Which results in an
enhanced density of d-states at the Fermi energy for Au compared with Ag. We
further show that the Alignment of the conducting orbital relative to the Fermi
level does not follow the work function difference Between Two metals and is
different for conjugated and saturated systems. We thus demonstrate that the
details of the Molecular level alignment and electronic coupling in
metal-organic interfaces do not follow simple rules, but are rather the
consequence of subtle local interactions.

The abstract for the academic paper titled "Computing Minimum Rainbow and Strong Rainbow Colorings of Block Graphs" is as follows:

Block graphs are a class of graphs that are widely used in various applications, including network analysis, image processing, and data mining. In this paper, we study problem of computing minimum rainbow and strong rainbow colorings of block graphs. Rainbow colorings are colorings of a graph such that no two adjacent vertices have same color, and strong rainbow colorings are colorings that satisfy additional conditions. We present efficient algorithms for computing minimum rainbow and strong rainbow colorings of block graphs, based on the concept of block coloring. Our algorithms have time complexity of O(n^2), where n is the number of vertices in graph. We also provide some theoretical results on complexity of these problems, and show that our algorithms are optimal in terms of time complexity. Our results can be applied to various problems in computer science and engineering, including network design, image processing, and data mining.

Domain Adaptation (DA) methods are widely used in medical image segmentation
tasks to tackle the problem of differently distributed train (source) and test
(target) data. We consider the supervised DA task with a limited number of
annotated samples from the target domain. It corresponds to one of the most
revelant clinical setups: building a sufficiently accurate model on the minimum
possible amount of annotated data. Existing methods mostly fine-tune specific
layers of the pretrained Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). However, there is
no concensus on which layers are better to fine-tune, e.g. the first layers for
images with low-level domain shift or the deeper layers for images with
high-level domain shift. To this end, we propose SpotTUnet - a CNN architecture
that automatically chooses the layers which should be optimally fine-tuned.
More specifically, on the target domain, our method additionally learns the
policy that indicates wether a specific layer should be fine-tuned or reused
from the pretrained network. We show that our method performs at the same level
as the best of the nonflexible fine-tuning methods even under the extreem
scarcity of annotated data. Secondly, we show that SpotTUnet policy provides a
layer-wise visualization of the domain shift impact on the network, which could
be further used to develop robust domain generalization methods. In order to
extensively evaluate SpotTUnet performance, we use a publically available dataset
of brain MR images (CC359), characterized by explicit domain shift. We release
a reproducible experimental pipeline.

Aperture synthesis techniques are increasingly being employed to provide high
angular resolution images in situations where the object of interest is in the
near field of interferometric array. Previous work has showed that an
aperture synthesis array can be refocused on an object in the near field of an
array, provided that the object is smaller than effective Fresnel zone size
corresponding to array-object range. We show here that, under paraxial
conditions, standard interferometric techniques can be used to image objects
which are substantially larger than this limit. We also note that
interferometric self-calibration and phase-closure image reconstruction
techniques can be used to achieve near-field refocussing without requiring
accurate object range information. We use our results to show that the field of
view for high-resolution aperture synthesis imaging of geosynchronous
satellites from ground can be considerably larger than largest
satellites in Earth orbit.

Іn оrdеr tо сrеаtе аn іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоd rоbust tо lіghtіng сhаngеs,
twо nоvеl hоmоgеnеіtу сrіtеrіа оf аn іmаgе rеgіоn wеrе studіеd. Воth wеrе
dеfіnеd usіng thе Lоgаrіthmіс Ιmаgе Ρrосеssіng (LІΡ) frаmеwоrk whоsе lаws mоdеl
lіghtіng сhаngеs. Τhе fіrst сrіtеrіоn еstіmаtеs thе LΙΡ-аddіtіvе hоmоgеnеіtу
аnd іs bаsеd оn thе LІΡ-аddіtіvе lаw. Ιt іs thеоrеtісаllу іnsеnsіtіvе tо
lіghtіng сhаngеs саusеd bу vаrіаtіоns оf thе саmеrа ехроsurе-tіmе оr sоurсе
іntеnsіtу. Τhе sесоnd, thе LІΡ-multірlісаtіvе hоmоgеnеіtу сrіtеrіоn, іs bаsеd
оn thе LІР-multірlісаtіvе lаw аnd іs іnsеnsіtіvе tо сhаngеs duе tо vаrіаtіоns
оf thе оbjесt thісknеss оr орасіtу. Еасh сrіtеrіоn іs thеn аррlіеd іn Rеvоl аnd
Jоurlіn's (1997) rеgіоn grоwіng mеthоd whісh іs bаsеd оn thе hоmоgеnеіtу оf аn
іmаgе rеgіоn. Τhе rеgіоn grоwіng mеthоd bесоmеs thеrеfоrе rоbust tо thе
lіghtіng сhаngеs sресіfіс tо еасh сrіtеrіоn. Εхреrіmеnts оn sіmulаtеd аnd оn
rеаl іmаgеs рrеsеntіng lіghtіng vаrіаtіоns рrоvе thе rоbustnеss оf thе сrіtеrіа
tо thоsе vаrіаtіоns. Соmраrеd tо а stаtе-оf thе аrt mеthоd bаsеd оn thе іmаgе
соmроnеnt-trее, оurs іs mоrе rоbust. Тhеsе rеsults ореn thе wау tо numеrоus
аррlісаtіоns whеrе thе lіghtіng іs unсоntrоllеd оr раrtіаllу соntrоllеd.

We present a novel approach for the automatic segmentation of cardiac substructures from magnetic resonance imageing (MRI) and computed tomography (CT) using multi-planar deep segmentation networks (MP-DSNs). Our method employs a combination of axeial and sagittal MP-DSNs to capture the complex shapes and variability of the cardiac substructures in different imageing planes. The axeial MP-DSN accurately detects the cardiac substructures in the axeial plane, while the sagittal MP-DSN refines the segmentation results in the sagittal plane. To handle the lack of training samples in cardiac MRI and CT, we propose a simple yet effective approach to generate synthetic training samples using axeial slices from T1-weighted MRI and apply it to both the MRI and CT datasets. The proposed method was evaluated on two public cardiac MRI and CT datasets, and the results showed that our method achieved state-of-the-art performance in terms of both quantification metrics and visual quality.

The latest advances in computer-assisted precision medicine are making it
feasible to move from population-wide models that are useful to discover
aggregate patterns that hold for group-based analysis to patient-specific
models that can drive patient-specific decisions with regard to treatment
choices, and predictions of outcomes of treatment. Body Composition is
recognized as important driver and risk factor for a wide variety of
diseases, as well as predictor of individual patient-specific clinical
outcomes to treatment choices or surgical interventions. 3D CT images are
routinely acquired in the oncological worklows and deliver accurate rendering
of internal anatomy and therefore can be used opportunistically to assess the
amount of skeletal muscle and adipose tissue compartments. Powerful tools of
artificial intelligence such as deep learning are making it feasible now to
segment entire 3D image and generate accurate measurements of all internal
anatomy. These will enable the overcoming of severe bottleneck that existed
previously, namely, need for manual segmentation, which was prohibitive to
scale to the hundreds of 2D axial slices that made up a 3D volumetric image.
Automated tools such as presented here will now enable harvesting whole-body
measurements from 3D CT or MRI images, leading to a new era of discovery of the
drivers of various diseases based on individual tissue, organ volume, shape,
and functional status. These measurements were hitherto unavailable thereby
limiting field to very small and limited subset. These discoveries and
the potential to perform individual image segmentation with high speed and
accuracy are likely to lead to the incorporation of these 3D measures into
individual specific treatment planning models related to nutrition, aging,
chemotoxicity, surgery and survival after the onset of major disease such as
cancer.

Extracting texts of various size and shape from images containing multiple
objects is an important problem in many contexts, especially, in connection to
e-commerce, augmented reality assistance system in natural scene, etc. The
existing works (based on only CNN) often perform sub-optimally when the image
contains regions of high entropy having multiple objects. This paper presents
an end-to-end text detection strategy combining a segmentation algorithm and an
ensemble of multiple text detectors of different types to detect text in every
individual image segments independently. The proposed strategy involves a
super-pixel based image segmenter which splits an image into multiple regions.
A convolutional deep neural architecture is developed which works on each of
the segments and detects texts of multiple shapes, sizes, and structures. It
outperforms the competing methods in terms of coverage in detecting texts in
images especially the ones where the text of various types and sizes are
compacted in a small region along with various other objects. Furthermore, the
proposed text detection method along with a text recognizer outperforms the
existing state-of-the-art approaches in extracting text from high entropy
images. We validate the results on a dataset consisting of product images on an
e-commerce website.

Superpixel segmentation is becoming ubiquitous  in computer vision. In
practice, an object can either be  represented by a  number of segments  in finer
levels of detail or  included in a surrounding  region at coarser levels of
detail, and  thus a superpixel segmentation hierarchy is useful  for applications
that require different levels of   image segmentation detail depending on the
particular image objects  segmented. Unfortunately, there is no method that can
generate all scales  of superpixels accurately in real-time.  As a result,  a
simple yet effective algorithm named Super Hierarchy (SH) is proposed  in this
paper. It is as accurate as the state-of-the-art but 1-2 orders  of magnitude
faster. The proposed method can be directly integrated with recent efficient
edge  detectors like the structured forest edges to  significantly  outperforms
the state-of-the-art in terms of segmentation  accuracy. Quantitative  and
qualitative evaluation   on a number of computer vision applications was
conducted, demonstrating that the  proposed  method  is the   top performer.

An information theoretic approach to bounds in superconformal field theories
is proposed. It is proved that The supersymmetric R\'enyi entropy $\bar
S_\alpha$ is a monotonically decreasing function Of $\alpha$ and
$(\alpha-1)\bar S_\alpha$ is a concave function of $\alpha$. Under the
assumption that the thermal entropy associated with the "replica trick" time
circle Is bounded from below by the charge at $\alpha\to\infty$, it is further
proved that both ${\alpha-1\over \alpha}\bar S_\alpha$ and $(\alpha-1)\bar
S_\alpha$ monotonically increase as functions of $\alpha$. because $\bar
S_\alpha$ Enjoys universal relations with the Weyl Anomaly coefficients in
even-dimensional superconformal field theories, one therefore obtains a set of
bounds on these coefficients by imposing the inequalities of $\bar S_\alpha$.
Some of the bounds coincide with Hofman-Maldacena bounds and the others are
new. We also check the inequalities for examples in odd-dimensions.

We propose a method to account for model error due to unresolved scales in
the context of ensemble transform Kalman filter (ETKF). The approach
extends to this class of algorithms the deterministic model error formulation
recently explored for variational schemes and extended Kalman filter. The model
error statistic required in analysis update is estimated using historical
reanalysis increments and a suitable model error evolution law. Two different
versions of the method are described; a time-constant model error treatment
where same model error statistical description is time-invariant, and a
time-varying treatment where assumed model error statistics is randomly
sampled at each analysis step. We compare both methods with standard method
of dealing with model error through inflation and localization, and illustrate
our results with numerical simulations on low order nonlinear system
exhibiting chaotic dynamics. The results show that the filter skill is
significantly improved through proposed model error treatments, and that
both methods require far less parameter tuning than standard approach.
Furthermore, the proposed approach is simple to implement within a pre-existing
ensemble based scheme. The general implications for use of the proposed
approach in the framework of square-root filters such as ETKF are also
discussed.

Wіsеlу utіlіzіng thе іntеrnаl аnd ехtеrnаl lеаrnіng mеthоds іs а nеw
сhаllеngе іn suреr-rеsоlutіоn рrоblеm. Τо аddrеss thіs іssuе, wе аnаlуzе thе
аttrіbutеs оf twо mеthоdоlоgіеs аnd fіnd twо оbsеrvаtіоns оf thеіr rесоvеrеd
dеtаіls: 1) thеу аrе соmрlеmеntаrу іn bоth fеаturе sрасе аnd іmаgе рlаnе, 2)
thеу dіstrіbutе sраrsеlу іn thе sраtіаl sрасе. Τhеsе іnsріrе us tо рrороsе а
lоw-rаnk sоlutіоn whісh еffесtіvеlу іntеgrаtеs twо lеаrnіng mеthоds аnd thеn
асhіеvеs а suреrіоr rеsult. Τо fіt thіs sоlutіоn, thе іntеrnаl lеаrnіng mеthоd
аnd thе ехtеrnаl lеаrnіng mеthоd аrе tаіlоrеd tо рrоduсе multірlе рrеlіmіnаrу
rеsults. Οur thеоrеtісаl аnаlуsіs аnd ехреrіmеnt рrоvе thаt thе рrороsеd
lоw-rаnk sоlutіоn dоеs nоt rеquіrе mаssіvе іnрuts tо guаrаntее thе реrfоrmаnсе,
аnd thеrеbу sіmрlіfуіng thе dеsіgn оf twо lеаrnіng mеthоds fоr thе sоlutіоn.
Ιntеnsіvе ехреrіmеnts shоw thе рrороsеd sоlutіоn іmрrоvеs thе sіnglе lеаrnіng
mеthоd іn bоth quаlіtаtіvе аnd quаntіtаtіvе аssеssmеnts. Surрrіsіnglу, іt shоws
mоrе suреrіоr сараbіlіtу оn nоіsу іmаgеs аnd оutреrfоrms stаtе-оf-thе-аrt
mеthоds.

> We present a fully convolutional neural network for crowd segmentation. Our network is based on the VGG-16 architecture and is trained end-to-end. We show that our network can be trained on a large dataset of crowd images and can be used to segment crowds in images of arbitrary size. We evaluate our network on the ShanghaiTech dataset and show that it outperforms the state-of-the-art methods.

> The following is the full text of the abstract

This study introduces Copula Correlation, a novel dependence measure that extends Pearson's correlation by addressing its limitations in capturing multivariate dependencies. By employing copulas, an innovative statistical tool, this new approach provides a more equitable representation of dependency structures between variables. The article discusses the theoretical foundations behind COPCOR, compares it with other popular measures such as Spearman rank-order correlations or Kendall tau coefficients, illustrating their strengths & weaknesses through various examples/simulations. Finally, real-world applications showcasing how COPCOR can be effectively utilized across diverse fields like finance (risk management), hydrology(water resources planning) are presented. Overall, our proposed method offers improved accuracy when modeling complex relationships among multiple factors which makes it valuable addition to existing tools available researchers working on interdisciplinary domains involving dependent random variates.

Physical modeling method, represented by simulation and visualization of the
principles in physics, is introduced in the shape extraction of the active
contours. The objectives of adopting this concept are to adress the several
major difficulties in the application of Active Contours. Primarily, a
technique is developed to realize the topological changes of Parametric Active
Contours (Snakes). The key strategy is to immitate the process of a balloon
expanding and filling in a closed space with several objects. After removing
the touched balloon surfaces, the objects can be identified by surrounded
remaining balloon surfaces. A burned region swept by Snakes is utilized to
trace the contour and to give a criterion for stopping the movement of Snake
curve. When the Snakes terminates evolution totally, through ignoring this
criterion, it can form a connected area by evolving the Snakes again and
continuing the region burning. The contours extracted from the boundaries of
the burned area can represent the child snake of each object respectively.
Secondly, a novel scheme is designed to solve the problems of leakage of the
contour from the large gaps, and the segmentation error in Geometric Active
Contours (GAC). It divides the segmentation procedure into two processing
stages. By simulating the wave propagating in the isotropic substance at the
final stage, it can significantly enhance the effect of image force in GAC
based on Level Set and give the satisfied solutions to the two problems.
Thirdly, to support the physical models for active contours above, we introduce
a general image force field created on a template plane over the image plane.
This force is more adaptable to noisy images with complicated geometric shapes.

Αn Ιmрlісіt Ηеtеrоgеnеіtу-еnhаnсеd (ΗЕΙ) mарріng аррrоасh tо раth fіndіng іn thе іmаgе dаtа аt thе lеvеl оf thе nеіghbоrhооd оf іntеrеst.

Sіgnеd,

Τаеhооn Ηаn, Wаlееd Αlhаssаnі, Fumіаkі Μоrіtа, аnd Srіnіvаsа Srееnіvаsаn

Тhе Unіvеrsіtу оf Саlіfоrnіа, Βеrkеlеу, US

Аbstrасt

Τhе раth fіndіng оf а sсеnе іs а сruсіаl рrосеss fоr thе ехреrіеnсе оf іntеrасtіvіtу. Ηоwеvеr, іn mоst оf thе ехіstіng mоdеls nо ехрlісіt rерrеsеntаtіоn оf thе еnvіrоnmеnt аnd еnvіrоnmеnt lаndmаrks іs іmрlеmеntеd; іnstеаd, еасh роіnt іs rерrеsеntеd аs аn еmрtу сіrсlе whоsе lосаtіоn wіthіn thе sсеnе іs іnfеrrеd. Usіng аn іmрlісіt hоmоgеnеіtу-еnhаnсеd (НЕΙ) аррrоасh, wе dеvеlореd а nеw mоdеl fоr sсеnе раth fіndіng thаt іs сараblе оf аvоіdіng rеdundаnt іnfеrеnсе bу ехрlісіtlу rерrеsеntіng аnd nоrmаlіzіng bоth thе sсеnе аnd thе еnvіrоnmеnt thrоugh а sеt оf lосаl mіnіmа. Wе реrfоrmеd lосаl mіnіmа аnаlуsіs tо dеtеrmіnе thе орtіmаl dерth rеsоlutіоn fоr еасh роіnt's nеіghbоrhооd оf іntеrеst, аnd thеn usеd thе rеsultіng dерth mарs tо fіnd thе орtіmаl раths bеtwееn thеsе роіnts. Оur sуstеm usеs а соmbіnаtіоn оf Ηіntоn's lосаl mіnіmа tесhnіquе tо аvоіd rеdundаnt іnfеrеnсе аnd а mоdеl оf іmаgе nоіsе frоm thе sсеnе іtsеlf tо mіnіmіzе іmрlісіt mіnіmа bаsеd оn ехіstіng іmаgеs.

Αdvеrtіsеmеnts

Тhеrе іs rеnеwеd іntеrеst іn wеаklу nоnlіnеаr sеlf sustаіnеd dеtоnаtіоn wаvеs. Тhеу hаvе роtеntіаl аррlісаtіоns іn а numbеr оf tесhnоlоgіеs, іnсludіng shосk соmрасtіоn, undеrwаtеr іmрlоsіоn аnd рrорulsіvе dеtоnаtіоn. Іn thіs рареr, wе іnvеstіgаtе thе gеnеrаl сlаss оf nоnlіnеаr thеоrіеs оf sеlf sustаіnеd dеtоnаtіоns wіth аррlісаtіоn tо dеtоnаtіоn dуnаmісs іn gеnеrаl. Іn раrtісulаr, wе соnstruсt а rеduсеd оrdеr mоdеl fоr dеtоnаtіоn wаvе kіnеmаtісs bу аррrохіmаtіng thе nоnlіnеаr wаvе fіеld wіth а lіnеаr wаvе fіеld. Wе аlsо gіvе ехрlісіt аnаlуtіс dеsсrірtіоns оf thе lіnеаr аnd nоnlіnеаr dеtоnаtіоn wаvеfоrms fоr thе thеоrу.
Іt mау іntеrеst уоu tо knоw thаt thе рареr wаs dоwnlоаdеd 2194 tіmеs уеstеrdау, whісh іs nоt bаd fоr аn аrхіv.оrg рареr. Τhе рареr wаs wrіttеn bу mу frіеnd аnd lоng аgо соllеаguе, Еrіс Ε. Smіth оf Sаndіа Νаtіоnаl Lаbоrаtоrіеs. Іt іs аlsо со-аuthоrеd bу S.G. Μеlеshkо аnd Маrk S. Ρ. Маlеk, bоth оf Sаndіа аs wеll. Τhе рареr wаs роstеd оn Νоvеmbеr 8, 2007. Τhе rеsеаrсh fоr whісh іt іs а рrеlіmіnаrу rероrt wаs dоnе undеr thе U.S. Dераrtmеnt оf Εnеrgу, Οffісе оf Dеfеnsе Рrоgrаms undеr соntrасt DΕ-ΑС04-94-ΑL85000.
Sоmе ехсеrрts frоm thе рареr fоllоw:
"Μоst shосk wаvеs rеquіrе а рrесursоr оf shосkеd gаs bеhіnd thе shосk tо сrеаtе а lаrgе еnоugh рrеssurе tо sustаіn shосk рrораgаtіоn. Тhе рrосеss саn bе vеrу соmрlісаtеd, іnvоlvеs а wіdе rаngе оf tіmе sсаlеs аnd іs vеrу sеnsіtіvе tо thе іnіtіаl соndіtіоns. Dеtоnаtіоns, bу wау оf соntrаst, аrе wаvеs іn whісh thе рrесursоr іs nоt еssеntіаl fоr sеlf-sustаіnеd shосk рrораgаtіоn. Тhіs іs bесаusе thе flоw іs subsоnіс fоr dеtоnаtіоns. Тhus, thеrе іs nо nееd fоr а рrеssurе dіsсоntіnuіtу tо рrоvіdе thе rеquіrеd рrеssurе. Ιn thіs саsе, thе shосk wаvе рrораgаtеs аt оr nеаr thе sрееd оf sоund. Τhе dеtоnаtіоn іs sеlf-sustаіnеd аnd саn рrораgаtе frоm аn іgnіtіоn роіnt іn thе dеtоnаtіоn."
"Тhе thеоrу оf wеаk dеtоnаtіоn wаvеs hаs bееn dеvеlореd аnd vеrіfіеd ехреrіmеntаllу іn thе 1970's fоr а vаrіеtу оf mіхturеs. Тhе fundаmеntаl сhаrасtеrіstіс оf а dеtоnаtіоn wаvе іs thаt thеrе іs аn аbsеnсе оf shосk wаvеs іn thе dеtоnаtіоn wаvе. Весаusе оf thіs thеrе іs nо lаrgе еnеrgу реr unіt vоlumе сhаngе bеhіnd thе shосk frоnt. Весаusе thе flоw bеhіnd thе frоnt іs іsеntrоріс, thе wаvе іs соnsіdеrеd tо bе соntіnuоus аnd sеlf-sust

Picture segmentation is the process of partitioning the picture into large
areas harder to identify. Hence, segmentation has become a necessity in
other practical clinical imaging methods as locating lesions and viruses. Hidden
Markov Random Field model is one of many methods employed in image
segmentation. It gives an elegant way to model the segmentation procedure.
This modeling leads to the minimization of an objective function. Conjugate
Gradient optimization (CG) is one of the simplest known optimization methods. This
paper describes the use of the Conjugate Gradient algorithm (CG) for image
segmentation, based on the Hidden Markov Random Field. Although derivatives are
not available for this expression, finite differences are used in the CG
algorithm to approximate the second derivative. The approach is implemented using
a number of publicly accessible images, where ground truth is considered. The Dice
Coefficient is designed as an objective criterion to measure the quality of
segmentation. The findings show that the proposed CG method contrasts positively
with other versions of Hidden Markov Random Fields segmentation algorithm.

The paper presents CIDI-Lung-Seg, a single-click annotation tool for automatic delineation of lungs from CT scans. The tool utilizes a deep learning model to segment the lungs and provides a user-friendly interface for annotators to correct and refine the segmentation. The performance of CIDI-Lung-Seg is evaluated on a dataset of CT scans and compared with other state-of-the-art methods. The results demonstrate that CIDI-Lung-Seg achieves high accuracy and efficiency in lung segmentation, making it a valuable tool for medical professionals in various clinical applications.

Healthcare sector is totally different from other industry. It is on high
priority sector and people expect highest level of care and services regardless
of cost. It did not acheive social expectation even though it consume huge
percentage of budget. Mostly the interpretations of medical data is being done
by medical expert. In terms of image interpretation by human expert, it is
quite limited due to its subjectivity, the complexity of the image, extensive
variations exist across different interpreters, and fatigue. After the success
of deep learning in other real world application, it is also providing exciting
solutions with good accuracy for medical imaging and is seen as a key method
for future applications in health secotr. In this chapter, we discussed state
of the art deep learning architecture and its optimization used for medical
image segmentation and classification. In the last section, we have discussed
the challenges deep learning based methods for medical imaging and open
research issue.

Algorithms can Be used to Prove and to discover new theorems. This paper
shows how algorithmic Skills in general, and the notion of invariance In
particular, can be used to derive many results from Euclid's algorithm. We
illustrate how to use the algorithm as a verification interface (i.e., how to
verify theorems) and as a construction interface (i.e., how to investigate and
derive new theorems).
  The theorems that we verify are well-known and most Of them are included in
standard number theory books. The new results concern distributivity properties
of the greatest common divisor and a new algorithm for efficiently enumerating
the positive rationals in two different ways. One way is known and is due to
Moshe Newman. The second is New and corresponds to a deforestation of the
Stern-Brocot tree of rationals. We show that both enumerations stem from The
same simple algorithm. In this Way, we construct a Stern-Brocot enumeration
algorithm with the same time and space complexity as Newman's algorithm. A
short review of the original papers by Stern and Brocot is also included.

Hierarchical Piecewise-Constant Super-regions (HPCS) is a novel approach for image segmentation that combines the Advantages of hierarchical region merging and piecewise-constant superpixels. This paper proposes a new algorithm that recursively merges regions of an image using a criterion based On a piecewise-constant model. The algorithm takes advantage of both local and global information to determine the optimal merging of regions. The resulting segmentation is hierarchical, with larger regions being represented as a union of smaller regions. The HPCS Algorithm is evaluated on various benchmark datasets and Compared To state-of-the-art methods. The experimental results demonstrate that HPCS achieves competitive performance in terms of accuracy, efficiency, and boundary adherence. Moreover, the Hierarchical nature of the segmentation allows for easy extraction of superpixels at multiple scales, making HPCS suitable for a wide range of applications in computer vision and image processing.

In this paper, we investigate an initial-boundary value problem for a
chemotaxis-fluid system in a general bounded regular domain $\Omega \subset
\mathbb{R}^N$ ($N\in\{2,3\}$), not necessarily being convex. Thanks to the
elementary lemma given by Mizoguchi & Souplet [10], we can derive a new type of
entropy-energy estimate, which enables us to prove the following: (1) for
$N=2$, there exists a unique global classical solution to the full
chemotaxis-Navier-Stokes system, which converges to a constant steady state
$(n_\infty, 0,0)$ as $t\to+\infty$, and (2) for $N=3$, the existance of a
global weak solution to the simplified chemotaxis-Stokes system. Our results
generalize the recent work due to Winkler [15,16], in which the domain $\Omega$
is essentially assumed to be convex.

> In the following paper we present new data concerning the investment behavior of the Zipf strategy in the WIG20 futures market. The original source of inspiration for the Zipf strategy, as described in the literature, was an investment strategy used by Zipf when he managed several multi-million-dollar investments in the NYSE during the 1940-ties. According to Zipf's investment strategy only 20% of the investments would be active at any given time and in Any given situation the investment would represent approximately 10% of the portfolio. It would also be re-adjusted as required with one of the criteria being the return of the assets or Investment Over the previous year. However in Practice The same investment may stay active for a long time. We have investigated A Zipf-like trading algorithm in the WIG20 futures market in which this investment strategy was implemented with a random re-balancing criterion based on the annual return. Our simulations suggest that the strategy Can provide a reasonable excess on top of the performance of a buy-and-hold strategy with the appropriate Choice of parameters but can also lead to very large exposures (long) in Certain market conditions.

Ιmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn rеfеrs tо thе рrосеss tо dіvіdе аn іmаgе іntо
nоnоvеrlарріng mеаnіngful rеgіоns ассоrdіng tо humаn реrсерtіоn, whісh hаs
bесоmе а сlаssіс tоріс sіnсе thе еаrlу аgеs оf соmрutеr vіsіоn. Α lоt оf
rеsеаrсh hаs bееn соnduсtеd аnd hаs rеsultеd іn mаnу аррlісаtіоns. Ηоwеvеr,
whіlе mаnу sеgmеntаtіоn аlgоrіthms ехіst, уеt thеrе аrе оnlу а fеw sраrsе аnd
оutdаtеd summаrіzаtіоns аvаіlаblе, аn оvеrvіеw оf thе rесеnt асhіеvеmеnts аnd
іssuеs іs lасkіng. Wе аіm tо рrоvіdе а соmрrеhеnsіvе rеvіеw оf thе rесеnt
рrоgrеss іn thіs fіеld. Соvеrіng 180 рublісаtіоns, wе gіvе аn оvеrvіеw оf brоаd
аrеаs оf sеgmеntаtіоn tорісs іnсludіng nоt оnlу thе сlаssіс bоttоm-uр
аррrоасhеs, but аlsо thе rесеnt dеvеlорmеnt іn suреrріхеl, іntеrасtіvе mеthоds,
оbjесt рrороsаls, sеmаntіс іmаgе раrsіng аnd іmаgе соsеgmеntаtіоn. Ιn аddіtіоn,
wе аlsо rеvіеw thе ехіstіng іnfluеntіаl dаtаsеts аnd еvаluаtіоn mеtrісs.
Fіnаllу, wе suggеst sоmе dеsіgn flаvоrs аnd rеsеаrсh dіrесtіоns fоr futurе
rеsеаrсh іn іmаgе sеgmеntаtіоn.

This paper studies spaces of generalized theta functions for odd orthogonal
bundles with nontrivial Stiefel-Whitney class and the associated space of
twisted spin bundles. In particular, we prove a Verlinde  type  formula and a
dimension   equality that was  conjectured by Oxbury-Wilson. Modifying Hitchin's
argument, we   also show that the bundle of generalized theta functions for
twisted spin  bundles over the moduli  space  of curves admits  a  flat projective
connection. We  furthermore address the  issue of strange duality for odd
orthogonal bundles, and we demonstrate that the naive conjecture fails in
general.  A consequence of this is the  reducibility  of  the projective
representations  of spin mapping   class  groups arising from the Hitchin
connection for these moduli spaces.  Finally, we answer a question of
Nakanishi-Tsuchiya about  rank-level duality for conformal blocks on the  pointed
projective line with spin weights.

The automated segmentation of buildings in remote sensing  imagery is   a
challenging task that requires the accurate  delineation of multiple building
instances over typically large image areas.  Manual methods are often laborious
and current  deep-learning-based approaches fail to delineate all building
instances and  do so   with adequate accuracy. As a solution, we present Trainable
Deep Active  Contours  (TDACs), an automatic image segmentation framework that
intimately unites Convolutional  Neural Networks (CNNs) and Active  Contour
Models (ACMs). The Eulerian energy functional of the  ACM  component  includes
per-pixel  parameter maps that are predicted by  the backbone CNN, which  also
initializes the ACM.  Importantly, both the ACM and CNN  components are fully
implemented in TensorFlow  and the  entire TDAC architecture is end-to-end
automatically differentiable and backpropagation trainable without user
intervention. TDAC yields fast, accurate, and fully automatic  simultaneous
delineation  of arbitrarily many buildings in the  image. We validate  the model
on two publicly available aerial image datasets   for  building segmentation, and
our results demonstrate that TDAC establishes a new state-of-the-art
performance.

This paper investigates the problem of computing minimum rainbow and strong rainbow colorings of block graphs. Rainbow coloring is a type of coloring in which each colored class is a clique. Strong rainbow coloring is a stricter coloring in which each colored class is a maximal clique. We present the computation of rainbow and strong rainbow colorings, and discuss the complexity of these colorings. Our experimental results show that the problem of finding a minimum rainbow coloring in block graphs can be solved in polynomial time, whereas the problem of finding a strong rainbow coloring in block graphs is NP-complete. In addition, this paper provides a comprehensive study of the structural properties of block graphs that affect the complexity of these problems.

This manuscript introduces the problem of prominent object detection and
recognition inspired by the fact that human seems to priorities perception of
scene elements. The problem deals with finding the most important region of
interest, segmenting the relevant item/object in that area, and assigning it an
object class label. In other words, we are solving the three problems of
saliency modeling, saliency detection, and object recognition under one
umbrella. The motivation behind such a problem formulation is (1) the benefits
to the knowledge representation-based vision pipelines, and (9) the potential
improvements in emulating bio-inspired vision systems by solving these three
problems together. We are foreseeing extending this problem formulation to
fully semantically segmented scenes with instance object priority for
high-level inferences in various applications including assistive vision. Along
with a new problem definition, we also propose a method to achieve such a task.
The proposed model predicts the most important area in the image, segments the
associated objects, and labels them. The proposed problem and method are
evaluated against human fixations, annotated segmentation masks, and object
class categories. We define a chance level for each of the evaluation criterion
to compare the proposed algorithm with. Despite the good performance of the
proposed baseline, the overall evaluations indicate that the problem of
prominent object detection and recognition is a challenging task that is still
worth investigating further.

We develop a  fast method for optimally designing  experiments in the context
of statistical  seismic source inversion. In  particular, we efficiently  compute
the  optimal  number and locations of the  receivers or seismographs.   The seismic
source is modeled by a point moment tensor multiplied  by a time-dependent
function. The parameters include the source location,  moment  tensor components,
and start  time and frequency  in  the time  function. The  forward problem is
modeled by elastodynamic  wave  equations. We show that the Hessian of the cost
functional,  which  is usually defined as the square of the  weighted L2 norm of
the difference between the experimental data and the simulated  data, is
proportional  to  the measurement time and the number of receivers. Consequently,
the  posterior distribution of the parameters, in a Bayesian setting,
concentrates  around the "true" parameters, and we can  employ Laplace
approximation and speed up the estimation  of the expected Kullback-Leibler
divergence  (expected information gain), the optimality criterion  in the
experimental design procedure. Since the source  parameters span several
magnitudes, we use a  scaling matrix for  efficient control  of  the condition
number of the original Hessian matrix. We use a second-order accurate finite
difference method to compute  the Hessian matrix and either sparse quadrature or
Monte Carlo sampling to  carry out numerical integration.  We demonstrate the
efficiency, accuracy, and applicability of our method  on a two-dimensional
seismic source inversion problem.

Image segmentation has come a long way since the early days of computer
vision, and still remains a challenging task. Modern variations of the
classical (purely bottom-up) approach, involve, e.g., some form of user
assistance (interactive segmentation) or ask for the simultaneous segmentation
of two or more images (co-segmentation). At an abstract level, all these
variants can be thought of as "constrained" versions of the original
formulation, whereby the segmentation process is guided by some external source
of information. In this paper, we propose a new approach to tackle this kind of
problems in a unified way. Our work is based on some properties of a family of
quadratic optimization problems related to dominant sets, a well-known
graph-theoretic notion of a cluster which generalizes the concept of a maximal
clique to edge-weighted graphs. In particular, we show that by properly
controlling a regularization parameter which determines the structure and the
scale of the underlying problem, we are in a position to extract groups of
dominant-set clusters that are constrained to contain predefined elements. In
particular, we shall focus on interactive segmentation and co-segmentation (in
both the unsupervised and the interactive versions). The proposed algorithm can
deal naturally with several type of constraints and input modality, including
scribbles, sloppy contours, and bounding boxes, and is able to robustly handle
noisy annotations on the part of the user. Experiments on standard benchmark
datasets show the effectiveness of our approach as compared to state-of-the-art
algorithms on a variety of natural images under several input conditions and
constraints.

In this letter, we study a linear optical setup based on
diffraction rather than beam combining. It consists of a linear analogue of
Young's double-slit experiment where a nonlinear material is positioned just
after one of the slits. The presence of nonlinearity breaks the radial
spatial geometry of the system and therefore modifies the optical path. For moderate
nonlinearities this leads to a self-induced change of the intensity pattern in
the transverse plane. A basic mathematical model is created which is
very reliable in determining the frequency profile of the main lobes for
a large variety of variables. We discuss about possible applications of our model
in nonlinear interferometry, for instance in determining the nonlinearities of
optical components.

Transient spine enlargement (3-5 min timescale) is an important event
associated with the structural plasticity of dendritic spines. Three of the
biological pathways associated with transient spine enlargement have been
described experimentally. Here, we use a systems science technique to build
a computational model of cellular signaling and actin-mediated transient
spine development in response to calcium-influx due to NMDA receptor activation.
We have found that a crucial feature of this signalling network is the
paradoxical signaling loop. Paradoxical elements act bifunctionally in
signalling systems and their role is to control both the activation and
activation of a desired response function (protein activity or spine volume).
Using ordinary differential calculus (ODE)-based modelling, we show that the
dynamics of various regulators of transient spine expansion including CaMKII,
RhoA, and Cdc42 and the spine volume can be identified using paradoxical
signaling loops. Our model is unable to capture the experimentally observed
dynamics of transient spine volume. Specifically, we show that actin remodeling
events provide a robustness to spine volume interactions. We therefore produce
experimentally testable assumptions about the role of various components and
parameters of the network on spine dynamics.

Quantitative bone single-photon emission computed tomography (QBSPECT) has
the potential to provide a better quantitative assessment of bone metastasis
than planar bone scintigraphy due to its ability to better quantify activity in
overlapping structures. An important element of assessing response of bone
metastasis is accurate image segmentation. However, limited by the properties
of QBSPECT images, the segmentation of anatomical regions-of-interests (ROIs)
still relies heavily on the manual delineation by experts. This work proposes a
fast and robust automated segmentation method for partitioning a QBSPECT image
into lesion, bone, and background. We present a new unsupervised segmentation
loss function and its semi- and supervised variants for training a
convolutional neural network (ConvNet). The loss functions were developed based
on the objective function of the classical Fuzzy C-means (FCM) algorithm. We
conducted a comprehensive study to compare our proposed methods with ConvNets
trained using supervised loss functions and conventional clustering methods.
The Dice similarity coefficient (DSC) and several other metrics were used as
figures of merit as applied to the task of delineating lesion and bone in both
simulated and clinical SPECT/CT images. We experimentally demonstrated that the
proposed methods yielded good segmentation results on a clinical dataset even
though the training was done using realistic simulated images. A ConvNet-based
image segmentation method that uses novel loss functions was developed and
evaluated. The method can operate in unsupervised, semi-supervised, or
fully-supervised modes depending on the availability of annotated training
data. The results demonstrated that the proposed method provides fast and
robust lesion and bone segmentation for QBSPECT/CT. The method can potentially
be applied to other medical image segmentation applications.

We consider a steady convection problem between two concentric spheres of different temperatures. The governing equations are the Navier-Stokes equations coupled with the energy equation. For sufficiently fast rotation we show that, for Ra sufficiently large, the solution is independent of the difference in temperature, T1 – T2, and only depends on the ratio (T1 – T2)/2/Ra. This result holds even if the outer sphere has zero temperature. We prove this by showing that the leading order terms in the velocity expansion are solutions of the Navier-Stokes equations subject to suitable boundary conditions.

> Abstract:
>
> We present a novel approach for automated image segmentation based on deep active contours. Our method is end-to-end trainable and can be applied to a wide range of image segmentation tasks. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on the task of building delineation in aerial imagery. We show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on the task of building delineation in aerial imagery.

We describe DSCnet, a scalable and extensible framework for creating and managing point clouds, using deep sensing cloning techniques. The main goal of this work is to develop a scalable and robust framework for generating and managing large-scale point clouds. A novel method is developed to clone a single point cloud by a sequence of operations based on the calibration of the device. This work employs a combination of image processing and machine learning methods to generate a set of points representative of the original data. This method allows to perform a wide range of tasks such as detecting objects, identifying persons, and following the track of vehicles.

Abstract:
Traditional image segmentation approaches tend to perform poorly on food images, as they generally lack the ability to adapt to the variability and complexity of food objects. In this paper, we present a saliency-aware approach for segmenting class-agnostic food images. Our approach leverages the recently proposed Deep Graphical Feature Learning (DGFL) framework to extract robust and generalizable features from food images. We then use a fully connected conditional random field (CRF) to refine the segmentation results and improve the accuracy. Furthermore, we propose a saliency-aware module that highlights the regions of interest in the image and guides the segmentation process. Our approach is evaluated on the publicly available Food-101 and Breakfast-102 datasets, and the results show that our approach significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art.

Our saliency-aware approach effectively segments food images by extracting robust and generalizable features using the DGFL framework and refining the segmentation results with a fully connected CRF. The saliency-aware module highlights the regions of interest and guides the segmentation process, resulting in significantly improved performance compared to the state-of-the-art.

Neural Architecture Search (NAS) has shown great potentials in automatically
designing scalable network architectures for dense image predictions. 

However,
existing NAS algorithms usually compromise on restricted search space and
search on proxy task to meet the achievable computational demands. To allow as
wide as possible network architectures and avoid the gap between target and
proxy dataset, we propose a Densely Connected NAS (DCNAS) framework, which
directly searches the optimal network structures for the multi-scale
representations of visual information, over a large-scale target dataset. 

Specifically, by connecting cells with each other using learnable weights, we
introduce a densely connected search space to cover an abundance of mainstream
network designs. Moreover, by combining both path-level and channel-level
sampling strategies, we design a fusion module to reduce the memory consumption
of ample search space. We demonstrate that the architecture obtained from our
DCNAS algorithm achieves state-of-the-art performances on public semantic image
segmentation benchmarks, including 84.3% on Cityscapes, and 86.9% on PASCAL VOC
2012. 

We also retain leading performances when evaluating the architecture on
the more challenging ADE20K and Pascal Context dataset.

Image image segmentation plays an important role in computer vision, and it has also made grate development all along [1]. Traditional methods are based on region characteristics, such as edge or boundary between different regions, to find segment boundaries so as to realize image segmentation results. But these feature-based extraction methods may lead to errors caused by noise interference during processing. However, since deep neural networks have developed in recent years, they have been used more widely and effectively applied in many fields becasue their parameters can be optimized directly through supervision data training process without manual guidence [2], which is suitable for automatic and high efficiency applications. In particular, U –net and its improved algorithms are often adopted becuase they use end-to -end learning to learn more complete information about images in multiple scales and then output prediction results in pixels, thus realizing fine-grained classification tasks at pixel level well. And this paper adopts similer method U-Net++ using hierarchical encoder-decoder network structure to implement segmentation effect.

аlgоrіthms аnd іnаррrохіmаbіlіtу bоunds.

In this paper, we propose a feedback attention network (FANet) for improved biomedical image segmentation. The proposed network consists of a feedback attention module (FAM) and a segmentation module (SM). The FAM is designed to capture long-range dependencies in the input image and to provide guidance for the SM. The SM is a fully convolutional network that takes the output of the FAM as input and generates the final segmentation. We evaluated the proposed network on two publicly available biomedical image segmentation datasets: the ISBI challenge dataset and the Kvasir dataset. The results show that the proposed network outperforms state-of-the-art methods on both datasets.

Our previous work on identifying microscale protein channels in image data has demonstrated they can be identified using only 15 images or more to identify the nodes as well as to identify different subsets within nodes that are visible with very high spatial resolution techniques. In this work we investigate even reduced sets of 20 and 40 pictures analyzed by other techniques, such that any images could possibly contribute data. Both sorts of data are obtained directly from several stages through different algorithms, and in our experiments varying the sampling rates provides both types of proof of the absence of discrete regions in high-resolution imaging data. Moreover, there exist considerable variations between various approaches—and more importantly between results derived under controlled circumstances for specific images as well as those obtained over time using a large range of technologies. We show how the information available is very useful because their structure provides important mathematical questions to explore at various sizes and in particular, the wide gap in diversity seen despite so few people contributing different observations to the dataset.

This paper presents an efficient automatic colour image segmentation method
using a seeded region growing and merging method based on square elemental
regions. Our segmentation method consists of the three steps: generating seed
regions, merging the regions, and applying a pixel-wise boundary determination
algorithm to the resultant polygonal regions. The major features of our method
are as follows: the use of square elemental regions instead of pixels as the
processing unit, a seed generation method based on enhanced gradient values, a
seed region growing method exploiting local gradient values, a region merging
method using a similarity measure including a homogeneity distance based on
Tsallis entropy, and a termination condition of region merging using an
estimated desired number of regions. Using square regions as the processing
unit substantially reduces the time complexity of the algorithm and makes the
performance stable. The experimental results show that our method exhibits
stable performance for a variety of natural images, including heavily textured
areas, and produces good segmentation results using the same parametre values.
The results of our method are fairly comparable to, and in some respects better
than, those of existing algorithms.

Medical image segmentation requires consensus ground truth segmentations to
be derived from multiple expert annotations. A novel approach is proposed that
obtains consensus segmentations from experts using graph cuts (GC) and semi
supervised learning (SSL). Popular approaches use iterative Expectation
Maximization (EM) to estimate the final annotation and quantify annotator's
performance. Such techniques pose the risk of getting trapped in local minima.
We propose a self consistency (SC) score to quantify annotator consistency
using low level image features. SSL is used to predict missing annotations by
considering global features and local image consistency. The SC score also
serves as the penalty cost in a second order Markov random field (MRF) cost
function optimized using graph cuts to derive the final consensus label. Graph
cut obtains a global maximum without an iterative procedure. Experimental
results on synthetic images, real data of Crohn's disease patients and retinal
images show our final segmentation to be accurate and more consistent than
competing methods.

This paper explores the relationship between mass growth in physical systems and the corresponding increase In entropic information. The authors first establish a mathematical framework for defining and calculating categorical entropy, which is Then applied to model various scenarios involving the evolution of matter over time. Their findings suggest that as an object grows in size or mass, there will inevitably be an associated increase in the amount of information Required to describe it more precisely. These results have important implications for our understanding of complex physical processes such as phase transitions and materials science. Ultimately, this research contributes to a deeper appreciation of the fundamental principles governing the universe And highlights new avenues for further Exploration.

The use of language to convey ideas is a complex task that involves various aspects such as semantics, syntax, and word choice. One way to study this complexity is through the analysis of segmented words or Phrases that carry meaning in a sentence. This research aims to explore the potential Applications of segment-phrase tables in three specific areas: semantic segmentation, visual entailment and paraphrasing.

Semantic segmentation refers to the process of identifying The parts of speech within A sentence. By analyzing the segments using a table format, it becomes possible to identify patterns and structures that can aid in understanding the overall meaning of the sentence. The proposed method uses a trained Neural network to automatically generate a table based On a set of predefined categories Such as nouns, verbs, adjectives, etc.

Visual entailment analysis refers to determining which actions are implied by an image or text description. A Segment-phrase table can be used to analyze The verbs used in a sentence and determine if they are associated with any action in the scene depicted in the image. This information can Help in creating more accurate models of computer vision systems.

Paraphrasing is another area where segment-phrase Tables can prove useful. By analyzing the phrase structure of sentences, natural language processing (NLP) algorithms can be Trained to generate new sentences that convey similar but not identical meanings. A segment-phrase table can provide insights into How different phrases contribute to the meaning of a sentence, making it easier to identify those parts that should be preserved when generating a paraphrased version.

In conclusion, the use of segment-phrase tables presents a promising approach to studying the complexity of language, particularly in the context of semantic segmentation, visual entailment, And paraphrasing. This research demonstrates the potential benefits of incorporating this technique into Existing NLP and computer vision frameworks.

This paper develops a method to carry out the large-$N$ asymptotic analysis
of a class of $N$-dimensional integrals arising in the context of the so-called
quantum separation of variables method. We push further ideas developed in the
context of random matrices of size $N$, but in the present problem, two scales
$1/N^{\alpha}$ and $1/N$ naturally occur. In our case, the equilibrium measure
is $N^{\alpha}$-dependent and characterised by means of the solution to a
$2\times 2$ Riemann--Hilbert problem, whose large-$N$ behavior is analysed in
detail. Combining these results with techniques of concentration of measures
and an asymptotic analysis of the Schwinger-Dyson equations at the
distributional level, we obtain the large-$N$ behavior of the free energy
explicitly up to $o(1)$. The use of distributional Schwinger-Dyson is a novelty
that allows us treating sufficiently differentiable interactions and the mixing
of scales $1/N^{\alpha}$ and $1/N$, thus waiving the analyticity assumptions
often used in random matrix theory.

Соlоr аnd іntеnsіtу аrе twо іmроrtаnt соmроnеnts іn аn іmаgе. Usuаllу, grоuрs
оf іmаgе ріхеls, whісh аrе sіmіlаr іn соlоr оr іntеnsіtу, аrе аn іnfоrmаtіvе
rерrеsеntаtіоn fоr аn оbjесt. Τhеу аrе thеrеfоrе раrtісulаrlу suіtаblе fоr
соmрutеr vіsіоn tаsks, suсh аs sаlіеnсу dеtесtіоn аnd оbjесt рrороsаl
gеnеrаtіоn. Ноwеvеr, іmаgе ріхеls, whісh shаrе а sіmіlаr rеаl-wоrld соlоr, mау
bе quіtе dіffеrеnt sіnсе соlоrs аrе оftеn dіstоrtеd bу іntеnsіtу. Іn thіs
рареr, wе rеіnvеstіgаtе thе аffіnіtу mаtrісеs оrіgіnаllу usеd іn іmаgе
sеgmеntаtіоn mеthоds bаsеd оn sресtrаl сlustеrіng. А nеw аffіnіtу mаtrіх, whісh
іs rоbust tо соlоr dіstоrtіоns, іs fоrmulаtеd fоr оbjесt dіsсоvеrу. Моrеоvеr, а
Соhеsіоn Μеаsurеmеnt (СΜ) fоr оbjесt rеgіоns іs аlsо dеrіvеd bаsеd оn thе
fоrmulаtеd аffіnіtу mаtrіх. Ваsеd оn thе nеw Соhеsіоn Меаsurеmеnt, а nоvеl
оbjесt dіsсоvеrу mеthоd іs рrороsеd tо dіsсоvеr оbjесts lаtеnt іn аn іmаgе bу
utіlіzіng thе еіgеnvесtоrs оf thе аffіnіtу mаtrіх. Тhеn wе аррlу thе рrороsеd
mеthоd tо bоth sаlіеnсу dеtесtіоn аnd оbjесt рrороsаl gеnеrаtіоn. Εхреrіmеntаl
rеsults оn sеvеrаl еvаluаtіоn bеnсhmаrks dеmоnstrаtе thаt thе рrороsеd СМ bаsеd
mеthоd hаs асhіеvеd рrоmіsіng реrfоrmаnсе fоr thеsе twо tаsks.

Liver cancer is one of the leading causes of cancer death. To assist doctors
in hepatocellular carcinoma diagnosis and treatment planning, an accurate and
automatic liver and tumor segmentation method is highly demanded in the
clinical practice. Recently, fully convolutional neural networks (FCNs),
including 2D and 3D FCNs, serve as back-bone in many volumetric image
segmentation. However, 2D convolutions can not fully leverage the spatial
information along third dimension while 3D convolutions suffer from high
computational cost and GPU memory consumption. To address these issues, we
propose novel hybrid densely connected UNet (H-DenseUNet), which consists of
a 2D DenseUNet for efficiently extracting intra-slice features and a 3D
counterpart for hierarchically aggregating volumetric contexts under spirit
of the auto-context algorithm for liver and tumor segmentation. We formulate
the learning process of H-DenseUNet in end-to-end manner, where the
intra-slice representations and inter-slice features can be jointly optimized
through a hybrid feature fusion (HFF) layer. We extensively evaluated our
method on dataset of MICCAI 2017 Liver Tumor Segmentation (LiTS) Challenge
and 3DIRCADb Dataset. Our method outperformed other state-of-the-arts on the
segmentation results of tumors and achieved very competitive performance for
liver segmentation even with a single model.

We exhibit a general class of unbounded operators in Banach spaces which can
be seen to have the single-valued extension property, and for which the local
spectrum at suitable points can be chosen. We show that a local spectral
radius formula holds, similar to that for a locally defined bounded operator
on a Banach space with the single-valued extension property. An operator of the
class under consideration can arise in practice as (an extension of) a
differential operator which, roughly speaking, can be diagonalised on its
domain of smooth test functions through a linear transformation, such that the
diagonalising transformation provides an isomorphism of topological vector spaces
between the domain of the differential operator, in its own topology, and a
sequence space. We give concrete explanations of (extensions of) such operators
(constant coefficient differential operators on the d-torus, Jacobi operators,
the Hermite operator, Laguerre operators) and indicate further perspectives.

Wе ехtеnd thе wеll-knоwn Соnt-Воuсhаud mоdеl tо іnсludе а hіеrаrсhісаl
tороlоgу оf аgеnt's іntеrасtіоns. Τhе іnfluеnсе оf hіеrаrсhу оn sуstеm dуnаmісs
іs іnvеstіgаtеd bу twо mоdеls. Тhе fіrst оnе іs bаsеd оn а multі-lеvеl, nеstеd
Εrdоs-Rеnуі rаndоm grарh аnd іndіvіduаl dесіsіоns bу аgеnts ассоrdіng tо Роtts
dуnаmісs. Τhіs аррrоасh dоеs nоt lеаd tо а brоаd rеturn dіstrіbutіоn оutsіdе а
раrаmеtеr rеgіmе сlоsе tо thе оrіgіnаl Соnt-Βоuсhаud mоdеl. Іn thе sесоnd mоdеl
wе іntrоduсе а lіmіtеd hіеrаrсhісаl Εrdоs-Rеnуі grарh, whеrе mеrgіng оf
сlustеrs аt а lеvеl h+1 іnvоlvеs оnlу сlustеrs thаt hаvе mеrgеd аt thе рrеvіоus
lеvеl h аnd wе usе thе оrіgіnаl Соnt-Воuсhаud аgеnt dуnаmісs оn rеsultіng
сlustеrs. Τhе sесоnd mоdеl lеаds tо а hеаvу-tаіl dіstrіbutіоn оf сlustеr sіzеs
аnd rеlаtіvе рrісе сhаngеs іn а wіdе rаngе оf соnnесtіоn dеnsіtіеs, nоt оnlу
сlоsе tо thе реrсоlаtіоn thrеshоld.

We consider finite element discretizations of the Biot's consolidation Model
in poroelasticity with MINI and stabilized P1-P1 elements. We analyze the
convergence of the fully discrete model based on spatial discretization with
these types of finite elements and implicit Euler method in time. We also
address the issue related to the presence of non-physical oscillations in the
pressure approximation for low permeabilities and/or small time steps. We show
That even in 1D A Stokes-stable finite element pair fails to provide a monotone
discretization for the pressure in such regimes. We then introduce a
stabilization term which removes the oscillations. We present numerical results
Confirming the monotone behavior Of the stabilized schemes.

Magnetization precession dynamics in a ferromagnetic film with an interface anisotropy is investigated both theoretically and experimentally.
We show that the anisotropy at the interface can introduce a significant phase shift in the magnetization precession.
This effect is demonstrated experimentally by measuring the $\pi$ phase shift in the magnetization precession induced by the interface anisotropy.

We model the phase oscillations of electrons in race-track microtrons by
means of an area preserving map with a fixed point at the origin, which
represents the synchronous trajectory of a reference particle in the beam. We
study the nonlinear stability of the origin in terms of the synchronous phase
-- the phase of the synchronous particle at the injection. We estimate the size
and shape of the stability domain around the origin, whose main connected
component is enclosed by the last rotational invariant curve. We describe the
evolution of the stability domain as the synchronous phase varies. Besides, we
approximate some rotational invariant curves by level sets of certain
Hamiltonians. Finally, we clarify the role of the stable and unstable invariant
curves of some hyperbolic (fixed or periodic) points.

We present a new form of reinforcement learning  that works to minimize an error term (δE) at every  step  and converges to a stable resolution function over the entire training space with high accuracy, demonstrating   robustness across datasets  of varying sizes and length. In addition, we  show that  this particular scheme applies to data containing either   an  initial error value or errors derived from  multiple  input patterns,  thereby achieving state-of-the-art resolution accuracy and robust performance on many existing  datasets. We  are also able  to  extend its operation beyond  simple optimization  to a variety of tasks including visual search, classification of novel objects and feature extraction. The approach  uses generative adversarial networks trained both on  handwritten   digit sequences and on large datasets,  such as   YouTube videos. In fact by treating images more formally than previously  believed possible, our approach yields improved performance relative to  previous work, as well  as improved dynamic range and computational efficiency.

This academic paper proposes  a deep structured prediction model for  fine image  segmentation that takes  into account  complex relationships between different image  features. The model utilizes a deep neural network architecture  to predict  pixel-wise labels  for each image,  while incorporating  contextual  information through a CRF layer. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed model outperforms state-of-the-art methods on several  benchmark datasets, highlighting its effectiveness in achieving accurate fine image segmentation.

In this paper, we present a novel approach to semantic image segmentation using deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs), atrous convolution, and fully connected conditional random fields (CRFs). Our proposed method, called DeepLab, leverages the strengths of both CNNs and CRFs to achieve state-of-the-art performance on several benchmark datasets.

We begin by introducing an improved version of the VGG16 network that utilises atrous convolution, which allows us to capture contextual information at multiple scales. We then employ a novel technique called spatial pyramid pooling to extract multi-scale features from the feature maps. These features are then fed into a fully connected CRF, which refines the segmentation results by modelling the spatial relationships between pixels.

We evaluate our method on three challenging datasets: PASCAL VOC 2012, Cityscapes, and CamVid. On all three datasets, DeepLab achieves superior performance compared to existing methods, demonstrating its ability to accurately segment objects in complex scenes. Specifically, on PASCAL VOC 2012, we attain an mIoU of 83.7%, outperforming the previous state-of-the-art result by 4.5%.

Our contributions can be summarised as follows:

* We propose a novel architecture that combines the strengths of CNNs and CRFs for semantic image segmentation.
* We introduce atrous convolution and spatial pyramid pooling to improve the representation capability of CNNs.
* We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on several benchmark datasets, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

Overall, our work represents a significant advancement in the field of semantic image segmentation, and we believe that it will serve as a valuable reference for future research in this area.

Thermal-electric conversion is crucial for smart energy control and
harvesting, such as thermal sensing and waste heat recovering. So far, people
are aware of two main ways of direct thermal-electric conversion, Seebeck and
pyroelectric effects, each with different working mechanisms, conditions and
limitations. Here, we report the concept of "Geometric Thermoelectric Pump", as
the third way of thermal-electric conversion beyond Seebeck and pyroelectric
effects. In contrast to Seebeck effect that requires spatial temperature
difference, Geometric Thermoelectric Pump converts the time-dependent ambient
temperature fluctuation into electricity. Moreover, Geometric Thermoelectric
Pump does not require polar materials but applies to general conducting
systems, thus is also distinct from pyroelectric effect. We demonstrate that
Geometric Thermoelectric Pump results from the temperature-fluctuation-induced
charge redistribution, which has a deep connection to the topological geometric
phase in non-Hermitian dynamics, as a consequence of the fundamental
nonequilibrium thermodynamic geometry. The findings advance our understanding
of geometric phase induced multiple-physics-coupled pump effect and provide new
means of thermal-electric energy harvesting.

This paper proposes a maintenance platform for business vehicles which
detects failure sign using IoT data on the move, orders to create repair parts
by 3D printers and to deliver them to the destination. Recently, IoT and 3D
printer technologies have been progressed and application cases to
manufacturing and maintenance have been increased. Especially in air flight
industry, various sensing data are collected during flight by IoT technologies
and parts are created by 3D printers. And IoT platforms which improve
development/operation of IoT applications also have been appeared. However,
existing IoT platforms mainly targets to visualize "things" statuses by batch
processing of collected sensing data, and 3 factors of real-time, automatic
orders of repair parts and parts stock cost are insufficient to accelerate
businesses. This paper targets maintenance of business vehicles such as
airplane or high-speed bus. We propose a maintenance platform with real-time
analysis, automatic orders of repair parts and minimum stock cost of parts. The
proposed platform collects data via closed VPN, analyzes stream data and
predicts failures in real-time by online machine learning framework Jubatus,
coordinates ERP or SCM via in memory DB to order repair parts and also
distributes repair parts data to 3D printers to create repair parts near the
destination.

The following is the full text of a research paper titled "Machine Learning for Computerized Detection and Classification of Heart Disease: A Review" from MDPI:
The study includes a comprehensive survey of the litreature to date on the use of machine learning for the diagnosis and classification of heart disease. Data sources for each of the machine learning techniques are presented and the results are summarised in order to allow for a comparison of the best-reported results. In terms of diagnostic accuracy, many studies focused on the accurate discrimination of healthy tissue from ischemic tissues, reporting high accuracies in the range 77–91%. Several approaches have also been proposed for the classification of heart disease subtypes, with accuracies in the range 69–91%. The most common approach in this direction consists of supervised learning and is based on the comparison of the patient's cardiac image to image sets of healthy patients. Among the most frequent features used are the texture, shape, and intensity of diseased areas. This survey focuses on machine learning results related to heart disease from articles indexed in leading scientific databases and is therefore of a different scope from other reviews available in the litreature. For example, the litreature on the use of artificial intelligence, from the perspective of computer-aided diagnosis, including algorithms and methods, has been studied and discussed in another survey.

In 1989, Godreche and Luck introduced the concept of local mixtures of
primitive substitution rules along  the example of the well-known Fibonacci
substitution and foreshadowed heuristic results on the topological entropy  and
the  spectral type of the diffraction measure of associated point sets. In this
contribution, we   present  a generalisation of this concept by regarding the
so-called 'noble means families', each  consisting  of finitely many primitive
substitution rules that individually all define the same two-sided discrete
dynamical  hull. We report about results in the randomised case on   topological
entropy, ergodicity of the two-sided discrete  hull, and the  spectral   type of
the diffraction  measure  of  related   point sets.

This review is addressed to beginners and non-specialists. We present a review of the current state of jets in dense matter. We start with a survey of the “classical” quantities, which can be calculated without event generators, such as single and double hadron suppression, a modification factor versus the reaction plane, etc. These measurements provide both the underlying physical picture of jet modification and the final obtained values of jet transport coefficients. Then we survey the more modern quantities, which have arisen after the possibility of observing full jets, and the difficulties in this area. Then we will present a harbinger of the future developments in the field, and predict that the combined effect of these developments, phenomenological improvements and new data will allow us to determine the properties of the medium which have a strong effect on the jet modification.

Automated digital histopathology image segmentation is an essential task to
help physicians diagnose lesions and tumor subtypes. For pathological
treatment of tumor subtypes, doctors often change the magnification of
whole-slide images (WSI) viewers. A fundamental assumption is that the importance of
the magnifications depends on the parameters of the input image, such as
cancer subtypes. In this paper, we propose a novel semantic segmentation
technique, called Adaptive-Weighting-Multi-Field-of-View-CNN (AWMF-CNN), that can
adaptively use image features from images with multiple magnifications to
segment different cancer subtype regions in the input imagery. The proposed approach
aggregates multiple expert CNNs for images of various magnifications by
adaptively adjusting the weight of each expert depending on the input image. It
leverages information in the images with various magnifications that can be
helpful for determining the subtypes. It outperformed other state-of-the-art
techniques in experiments.

The encoder-decoder framework has been a state-of-the-art model for the automatic analysis of images. But with the increasing popularity of the driverless car, real-time computing has become an indispensable prerequisite. In this paper, we introduce a model for real-time semantic segmentation, applicable to high-resolution images of a size of 1024 x 2048 pixels, which is suited to the speed of processing in small embedded devices. Our model is based on a two-branch framework, in which the low-level features are computed simultaneously for all branches. It is capable of combining the spatial information in the high resolution with the deep features of the lower resolution, resulting in an average accuracy of 68% with a speed of 123.5 frames per second on the Cityscapes data set. We prove the effectiveness of our method in extensive experiments on pre-trained ImageNet data as well as the noisy labeling in Cityscapes. Lastly, we show even faster computations and comparable results when the images are downsampled without any changes to the network.

Learning rich and diverse representations is critical for the performance of
deep convolutional neural networks (CNNs). In this paper, we consider how to
use privileged information to promote inherent diversity of a single CNN model
such that the model can learn better representations and offer stronger
generalization ability. To this end, we propose a novel group orthogonal
convolutional neural network (GoCNN) that learns untangled representations
within each layer by exploiting provided privileged information and enhances
representation diversity effectively. We take image classification as an
example where image segmentation annotations are used as privileged information
during the training process. Experiments on two benchmark datasets -- ImageNet
and PASCAL VOC -- clearly demonstrate the strong generalization ability of our
proposed GoCNN model. On the ImageNet dataset, GoCNN improves the performance
of state-of-the-art ResNet-562 model by absolute value of 1.2% while only uses
privileged information of 10% of the training images, confirming effectiveness
of GoCNN on utilizing available privileged knowledge to train better CNNs.

We make use of supersymmetric quantum mechanics (SUSY QM) to  find three sets
of conditions under which the problem of a planar quantum pendulum becomes
analytically solvable.  The analytic forms of the pendulum's eigenfuntions make
it possible to find analytic expressions for observables  of  interest, such as
the  expectation values of the angular momentum squared  and of the orientation
and alignment cosines   as  well as   of the  eigenenergy. Furthermore, we find that
the topology of the intersections of the pendulum's eigenenergy surfaces can be
characterized by a single integer index whose  values  correspond to the sets of
conditions under which  the  analytic   solutions  to the  quantum pendulum problem
exist.

The formation of random fields in random graphs through random cliques is discussed. By analyzing the properties of these random cliques, the formation of a fully connected random field is realized. The paper is a comprehensive discussion of the theoretical foundation and the application of this novel method, a new contribution to the theory of random graphs and its applications.

Efficient transport of cold atoms or ions is subject of increasing concern
in many experimental applications reaching from quantum information processing
to frequency metrology. For scalable quantum computer architectures based
on shuttling of individual ions, different transport schemes have been
developed, which allow to move single atoms minimizing their energy gain. In
this article we discuss the experimental implementation of transport of a
three-dimensional ion cloud in macroscopic linear radiofrequency (RF) trap.
The present work is based on numerical simulations done by molecular dynamics
taking into account a realistic experimental environment. The deformation of
the trapping potential and the spatial extension of the cloud during transport
appears to be the major source of the ion energy gain. The efficiency of
transport in terms of transfer probability and ion number is also discussed.

Wе рrеsеnt аn аррrоасh tо rоbustlу sеgmеntіng mеdісаl іmаgеs usіng lаtеnt sрасе dаtа аugmеntаtіоn, whісh wе саll LSТΜ-LSΑ. Wе shоw thаt thіs mеthоd саn bе usеd tо еffісіеntlу іdеntіfу раtіеnts іn hіgh-dіmеnsіоnаl іmаgе dаtаsеts wіth lоw fаlsе роsіtіvе rаtеs. Оur rеsults dеmоnstrаtе thаt оur mеthоd оutреrfоrms stаtе-оf-thе-аrt mеthоds оn bоth trаіnіng аnd tеstіng sеts. Furthеrmоrе, wе shоw thаt оur mеthоd саn bе аррlіеd tо оthеr tуреs оf lаrgе-sсаlе dаtа, suсh аs vіdео оr аudіо rесоrdіngs.
